_comment: SCA (software-composition-analysis) policy for DeltaSharp — STORY-00.3.1 (#107).
Consumed by tools/security/sca-gate.py and the 'sca' job in .github/workflows/security.yml.
See docs/engineering/design/supply-chain-security.md for the full policy narrative.
failOnSeverity: the gate FAILS CI when any dependency has a vulnerability at or above
this severity (one of: low, moderate, high, critical). Set to 'high' to match the
build-time NuGet audit posture (NU1903/NU1904 HIGH/CRITICAL are build-breaking;
NU1901/NU1902 LOW/MODERATE are warnings) declared in Directory.Build.props.
suppressions[]: an auditable waiver for an accepted/false-positive finding. EVERY entry
MUST carry package, scope, reason, owner, and expiry (YYYY-MM-DD). An EXPIRED suppression
stops suppressing — the finding re-surfaces and fails the gate — so waivers cannot rot.
Optional: versions ('*' or a comma-separated exact list), advisory (pin to one URL),
reviewCriteria (the condition to re-review instead of blanket expiry).
Example entry:
"package": "Some.Transitive.Package",
"advisory": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx",
"scope": "DeltaSharp.Core (transitive via Foo)",
"reason": "Not reachable from DeltaSharp code paths; upstream fix pending.",
"owner": "@khaines",
"reviewCriteria": "Remove when Foo >= 2.0 (fixed) is adopted."
expectedProjects: FAIL-CLOSED report-provenance allowlist — the project names that MUST
appear in `dotnet list DeltaSharp.sln package --vulnerable --format json`. That command
exits 0 with an EMPTY finding set for BOTH a genuinely clean run AND an unreachable
advisory DB / dropped project / truncated JSON, so 'no findings' alone is fail-OPEN. A
healthy report lists a path for EVERY solution project even when clean; the gate asserts
each name here is present (matched by project name, path/OS-independent) and FAILS CLOSED
naming any missing project. Mirrors coverage-config.json's expectedAssemblies. Keep this
in sync with the solution: add a project here when it lands (a deliberate governance event);
removing one that is still in the solution will (correctly) fail the gate. An empty list
still fails closed on a wholly empty report.
